titleStmt.title: SII 22.77: original edition by G.V. Srinivasa Rao
No. 77. (A.R. No. 77 of 1906) SOUTH ARCOT DISTRICT, TIRUKKŌYILŪR TALUK, JAMBAI. JAMBUNĀTHA TEMPLE—ON THE WEST WALL OF THE SAME SHRINE. RĀJARĀJA I—28th YEAR (A.D. 1012-13)

summary.p: The inscription begins with introduction Tirumagaḷpōla etc. It records a gift of 10 kaḷañju of gold deposited with the ūrār of Pālaippandal on the southern bank for a perpetual lamp in the temple by a merchant of the village by name Aṅgāḍi Poṟṟāmaṉ for (the memory of) one Pirāṭṭi Śīrāḷaṉ of Munaippāḍi Nāvalūr. The reason for the gift is stated to be that the latter trespassed into the former's house in the night and tried to assault his mistress in his very presence, by which he was enraged so much as to stab the intruder to death.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
svasti śrī . tirumakaḷpolap perunilaccelviyu n=taṉakke yurimai pūṇṭamai maṉakkoḷak kān=taḷūr
ccālai kala maṟuttaruḷi veṅkaināṭuṅ kaṅkapāṭiyu n=taṭikaipāṭiyu nuḷampa pāṭiyum kuṭamalaināṭum kolla
muṅ kaliṅkamu meṇṭicai pukaḻtara viḻamaṇṭalamu miraṭṭapāṭi yeḻarai yilakkamu n=tiṇṭiṟal veṉṟi taṇṭāṟ koṇ
ṭa taṉṉeḻil vaḷarūḻi yellāviyāṇṭu n=toḻutaka viḷaṅku miyāṇṭe ceḻiyarait tecukoḷ śrīkovirāca rāca rācakecari pa
nmarāṉa śrīrācarāca tevaṟ kiyāṇṭu 2 10 8Āvatu Ivvūr viyāpāri Aṅkāṭi poṟṟāmaṉ vaiccuk koṇṭirun=ta peṇṭāṭṭi
ci mātevi peṟṟiyai muṉaippāṭi nāvalūrilirukkum pirāṭṭi cirāḷaṉ Irāttiri cimātevi peṟṟiyai valiyappukku piṭi
Aṅkāṭi poṟṟāmaṉ Eṉ maṇavāṭṭiyai nāṉirukkave valiya piṭiccāṉeṉṟu pukku paṭak kuttiṉamaiyil Aṅkāṭi
poṟṟāmaṉmel vaḻakkaḻivu Ilāmaiyil pirāṭṭi cirāḷamāṭait toṭoruṅki pirāṭṭi cirāḷaṉukkāka Oru non=tāviḷakku
ttāṉtoṉṟiyāḷvārkku can=tirātittavaṟ nicata muḻakku Eṇṇai yaṭṭi viḷakkerikka Aṅkāṭi poṟṟāmaṉ kuṭutta po
ṉ patiṉ kaḻañcum koṇṭu nicata muḻakkeṇṇai yaṭṭuvomāṉo minnāṭṭu peṇṇait tenkarai pālaippan=talū
rom can=tirātittavaṟ panmāheśvararakṣai .
Digital edition of SII 22.77 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
